text:
There once lived a wise owl named Ethereal. She lived deep inside an old oak tree. One autumn day, she noticed a young chick tumbling out onto the earth. Intrigued, Ethereal swooped down.

Ethereal gently picked the chick up. "Where did you come from?" she asked.

"I slipped. Ow. That oak tree?" the chick cried.

"I think I could rescue you." Ethereal chirped.

Through careful flight paths that avoided the hawk shadows, Ethereal guided the chick back up.

That night Ethereal lulled the chick with stories. And the chick slept soundly.

In springtime, the chick grew. Ethereal taught it the dances of seasons. From snowflakes to blooming flowers. Together they flew.

Years passed. The chick grew wise just like Ethereal. They reminisced about their golden days. The owl showed wisdom. The chick shared stories.

One dusk, as stars twinkled overhead, Ethereal passed away. But
